name: Quartz

aliases.name: Iso
aliases.comment:
This is an `Optic` that represents an isomorphism. Two types are isomorphic
if you can convert between them without losing any information.
aliases.type: Quartz.Type.Iso.Iso p l s t a b

aliases.name: Lens
aliases.comment:
This is an `Optic` that can be used to represent a field accessor. Note
that it cannot be used as a prism.
aliases.type: Quartz.Type.Lens.Lens l s t a b

aliases.name: No
aliases.comment:
Equivalent to `Never`, the empty type. Like `Yes`, this is used to signal
that an `Optic` does not support a certain feature.

aliases.name: Optic
aliases.comment:
This is the base optic type. All other optics are defined in terms of this
type.

Don't be intimidated by all the type variables! Here's what they mean:

  - `p`: Does this optic support being used as a prism? This will either be
    `Yes` or `No`.

  - `l`: Does this optic support being used as a lens? Like `p`, this will
    either be `Yes` or `No`.

  - `s`: This is the input type. Often this is a "big" type like a record.

  - `t`: This is the output type. Often this is the same as the input type, but
    that's not required.

  - `a`: This is the input type of the focused element. Often this is a "small"
    type like one field in a record.

  - `b`: This is the output type of the focused element. Often this is the same
    as the input type, but that's not required.
aliases.type: Quartz.Type.Optic.Optic p l s t a b

aliases.name: Prism
aliases.comment:
This is an `Optic` that can be used to represent a constructor. Note that
it cannot be used as a lens.
aliases.type: Quartz.Type.Prism.Prism p s t a b

aliases.name: SimpleIso
aliases.comment: This is an `Iso` where the input and output types are the same.

aliases.name: SimpleLens
aliases.comment: This is a `Lens` where the input and output types are the same.

aliases.name: SimpleOptic
aliases.comment: This is an `Optic` where the input and output types are the same.

aliases.name: SimplePrism
aliases.comment: This is a `Prism` where the input and output types are the same.

aliases.name: SimpleTraversal
aliases.comment: This is a `Traversal` where the input and output types are the same.

aliases.name: Traversal
aliases.comment:
This is an `Optic` that may contain zero or many focused values. Note that
it cannot be used as a prism nor a lens.

aliases.name: Yes
aliases.comment:
Equivalent to `()`, the unit type. Used to signal that an `Optic` supports
a certain feature.

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Composes two `Optic`s. This is meant to be used in a pipeline. The first
argument is the inner `Optic` and the second argument is the outer `Optic`.

    preview (just |> andThen ok) <| Just <| Ok 1
    -- Just 1
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l u v a b -> Quartz.Optic p l s t u v -> Quartz.Optic p l s t a b

values.name: array
values.comment:
Focuses on each element of the `Array`.

    over array negate <| Array.fromList [ 1, 2 ]
    -- Array.fromList [ -1, -2 ]
values.type: Quartz.Traversal (Array.Array a) (Array.Array b) a b

values.name: elements
values.comment:
Converts between an `Array` and a `List`.

    view elements <| Array.fromList [ 1, 2 ]
    -- [ 1, 2 ]
values.type: Quartz.Iso p l (Array.Array a) (Array.Array b) (List.List a) (List.List b)

values.name: err
values.comment:
Focuses on the `Err` variant of a `Result`.

    review err "failure"
    -- Err "failure"
values.type: Quartz.Prism p (Result.Result a x) (Result.Result b x) a b

values.name: first
values.comment:
Focuses on the first element of a tuple.

    view first ( 1, 2 )
    -- 1
values.type: Quartz.Lens l ( a, x ) ( b, x ) a b

values.name: idx
values.comment:
Focuses on the element at the given index of the `Array`.

    preview (idx 1) <| Array.fromList [ 1, 2 ]
    -- Just 2
values.type: Basics.Int -> Quartz.SimpleTraversal (Array.Array a) a

values.name: is
values.comment:
Returns `True` if the given `Optic` matches, `False` otherwise. Typically
this is used as an alternative to `preview`.

    is just <| Just 1
    -- True
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l s t a b -> s -> Basics.Bool

values.name: iso
values.comment:
Use this function to create a new `Iso`. You have to provide conversion
functions for both directions.

    type Name
        = Name String

    nameIso : SimpleIso p l String Name
    nameIso =
        iso Name <| \(Name s) -> s
values.type: (s -> a) -> (b -> t) -> Quartz.Iso p l s t a b

values.name: just
values.comment:
Focuses on the `Just` variant of a `Maybe`.

    review just "success"
    -- Just "success"
values.type: Quartz.Prism p (Maybe.Maybe a) (Maybe.Maybe b) a b

values.name: key
values.comment:
Focuses on the given key in the `Dict`.

    preview (key "k") (Dict.singleton "k" "v")
    -- Just "v"
values.type: comparable -> Quartz.SimpleTraversal (Dict.Dict comparable a) a

values.name: lens
values.comment:
Use this function to create a new `Lens`. You have to provide a way to get
the part out of the whole, and a way to construct the whole with a new part.

    type alias Person =
        { name : String }

    nameLens : SimpleLens l Person Name
    nameLens =
        lens .name <| \person name -> { person | name = name }
values.type: (s -> a) -> (s -> b -> t) -> Quartz.Lens l s t a b

values.name: list
values.comment:
Focuses on each element of the `List`.

    over list negate [ 1, 2 ]
    -- [ -1, -2 ]
values.type: Quartz.Traversal (List.List a) (List.List b) a b

values.name: nothing
values.comment:
Focuses on the `Nothing` variant of a `Maybe`.

    review nothing ()
    -- Nothing
values.type: Quartz.SimplePrism p (Maybe.Maybe a) ()

values.name: nth
values.comment:
Focuses on the element at the given index of the `List`.

    preview (nth 1) [ 1, 2 ]
    -- Just 2
values.type: Basics.Int -> Quartz.SimpleTraversal (List.List a) a

values.name: ok
values.comment:
Focuses on the `Ok` variant of a `Result`.

    review ok "success"
    -- Ok "success"
values.type: Quartz.Prism p (Result.Result x a) (Result.Result x b) a b

values.name: over
values.comment:
Maps the given function over the `Optic`. If the `Optic` doesn't match,
nothing will happen. See `set` for a similar function that unconditionally sets
the value rather than updating it.

    over just negate <| Just 1
    -- Just -1
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l s t a b -> (a -> b) -> s -> t

values.name: pairs
values.comment:
Converts between a `Dict` and a `List` of pairs.

    view pairs <| Dict.singleton "k" "v"
    -- [ ( "k", "v" ) ]
values.type: Quartz.Iso p l (Dict.Dict comparable1 a) (Dict.Dict comparable2 a) (List.List ( comparable1, a )) (List.List ( comparable2, a ))

values.name: preview
values.comment:
Returns `Just` if the `Optic` matches, `Nothing` otherwise. See `toListOf`
for a similar function that can return multiple matches.

    preview ok <| Ok 1
    -- Just 1
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l s t a b -> s -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: prism
values.comment:
Use this function to create a new `Prism`. You have to provide a way to
call the constructor and a way to destructure a value.

    type Number
        = Integral Int
        | Floating Float

    iPrism : SimplePrism p Number Int
    iPrism =
        prism Integral <|
            \n ->
                case n of
                    Integral i ->
                        Ok u

                    _ ->
                        Err n
values.type: (b -> t) -> (s -> Result.Result t a) -> Quartz.Prism p s t a b

values.name: re
values.comment:
Use this function to flip an `Optic` around.

    stringToList : SimpleIso String (List Char)
    stringToList =
        iso String.toList String.fromList

    listToString : SimpleIso (List Char) String
    listToString =
        re stringToList
values.type: Quartz.Optic Quartz.Yes Quartz.Yes s t a b -> Quartz.Optic p l b a t s

values.name: review
values.comment:
Creates a new value using the given `Optic`.

    review just 1
    -- Just 1
values.type: Quartz.SimpleOptic Quartz.Yes l s a -> a -> s

values.name: second
values.comment:
Focuses on the second element of a tuple.

    view second ( 1, 2 )
    -- 2
values.type: Quartz.Lens l ( x, a ) ( x, b ) a b

values.name: set
values.comment:
Sets the small value inside the big value using the provided `Optic`. See
`over` for a similar function that allows modifying the value.

    set first 3 ( 1, 2 )
    -- ( 3, 2 )
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l s t a b -> b -> s -> t

values.name: swap
values.comment:
Swaps the first and second elements of a tuple.

    view swap ( 1, 2 )
    -- ( 2, 1 )
values.type: Quartz.Iso p l ( a, b ) ( c, d ) ( b, a ) ( d, c )

values.name: toListOf
values.comment:
Extracts all of the values that match the provided `Optic`. See `preview`
for a similar function that only returns zero or one value.

    toListOf array <| Array.fromList [ 1, 2 ]
    -- [ 1, 2 ]
values.type: Quartz.Optic p l s t a b -> s -> List.List a

values.name: traversal
values.comment:
Use this function to create a new `Traversal`. You have to provide a way to
convert the input into a `List`, as well as a way to map over values in the
input.

    data Pair a =
        Pair a a

    pairTraversal : SimpleTraversal (Pair a) a
    pairTraversal =
        traversal
            (\(Pair a1 a2) -> [ a1, a2 ])
            (\f (Pair a1 a2) -> Pair (f a1) (f a2))
values.type: (s -> List.List a) -> ((a -> b) -> s -> t) -> Quartz.Traversal s t a b

values.name: view
values.comment:
Extracts a value using the provided `Optic`. See `preview` for a similar
function that works with more types of `Optic`s.

    view first ( 1, 2 )
    -- 1
values.type: Quartz.Optic p Quartz.Yes s t a b -> s -> a
